assembly_instructions: Two-segment crewed spacecraft from the Apollo program. The conical Command Module (CM) sits on top of the cylindrical Service Module (SM), which ends in a bell-shaped main engine nozzle at its aft. A Launch Escape System tower rises from the CM apex during ascent; a docking probe at the CM apex is revealed after the LES is jettisoned. Four clusters of Reaction Control System (RCS) thruster quads ring the SM at 90° spacing; a steerable high-gain antenna, rectangular radiator panels, a fuel-cell compartment, and internal propellant tanks complete the Service Module. An access hatch opens on the CM side wall.

info.japanese_name: アポロ司令船・機械船 (CSM)
info.english_name: NASA Apollo Command and Service Module (CSM)
info.summary: Crewed spacecraft used on all Apollo lunar missions. A conical 3-seat Command Module rode home on the heat shield; a cylindrical Service Module carried propulsion, power, and life support.
info.description: The Apollo Command and Service Module (CSM) was the crew-carrying spacecraft of NASA's Apollo program. It consisted of two mated sections: the Command Module (CM), a pressurized conical capsule with an ablative heat shield at its base that housed three astronauts and returned them to Earth; and the Service Module (SM), a cylindrical stage behind the CM that carried the Service Propulsion System (SPS) main engine, four Reaction Control System (RCS) thruster quads, the high-gain antenna, radiator panels, fuel cells, and cryogenic propellant and reactant tanks. A Launch Escape System tower mounted above the CM during ascent could pull the capsule clear of a failing booster; once safe staging was achieved it was jettisoned, exposing the docking probe used to mate with the Lunar Module. NASA's Apollo mission documentation and engineering drawings are in the public domain.
info.contractor: North American Aviation / Rockwell (CM); North American Aviation (SM)
info.contract_date: Development contract 1961; flew Apollo 7 (Oct 1968) through Apollo-Soyuz (Jul 1975)
info.status: Retired; flown on 15 crewed Apollo/Skylab/ASTP missions

facts.label: Propulsion (SM)
facts.value: SPS bell-nozzle main engine + 4 × RCS quads

facts.label: Re-entry
facts.value: CM ablative heat shield at base of cone

facts.label: License
facts.value: NASA imagery / engineering docs — public domain

parts.name: Command Module (CM)
parts.role: Pressurized three-seat re-entry capsule with ablative heat shield at its base. Conical in reality; approximated here as a cylinder.

parts.name: Service Module (SM)
parts.role: Cylindrical service stage — carries the main engine, RCS, fuel cells, radiators, and propellant.

parts.name: SPS Main Engine Nozzle
parts.role: Service Propulsion System bell-shape main engine nozzle. Fired for lunar-orbit insertion and trans-Earth injection.

parts.name: Launch Escape System (LES)
parts.role: Solid-fuel escape rocket on a tower above the CM. Pulls the capsule clear of an exploding booster, then is jettisoned once safe staging is reached.

parts.name: Docking Probe
parts.role: Central probe at the CM apex; engages the Lunar Module's drogue for docking in lunar transit.

parts.name: RCS Thruster Quad (+X)
parts.role: One of four 90°-spaced Reaction Control System quads. Four small thrusters per quad give six-DOF attitude control.

parts.name: RCS Thruster Quad (−X)
parts.role: Reaction Control System quad on the −X side of the Service Module.

parts.name: RCS Thruster Quad (+Z)
parts.role: Reaction Control System quad on the +Z side of the Service Module.

parts.name: RCS Thruster Quad (−Z)
parts.role: Reaction Control System quad on the −Z side of the Service Module.

parts.name: Steerable High-Gain Antenna
parts.role: Dish-shaped steerable high-gain antenna for S-band communications with Earth. Rendered as a shallow disc.

parts.name: Radiator Panel
parts.role: Flat radiator panel wrapping part of the SM exterior — rejects waste heat from the fuel cells and environmental-control system.

parts.name: Fuel-Cell Compartment
parts.role: External access panel over the fuel-cell bay. H2 + O2 → electricity + water for the crew.

parts.name: Propellant Tank (internal)
parts.role: One of the SPS propellant tanks inside the Service Module. Partially embedded in the SM shell as an indicator of internal layout.

parts.name: CM Side Hatch
parts.role: Unified side hatch for crew ingress/egress.
